description: Top rework causes, foreman tips, new journeyman wisdom, toolbox talks, common miscommunications

top_rework_causes_electrical.cause: Cable pulled to wrong location or wrong cable pulled
top_rework_causes_electrical.prevention: Two-person verification of cable tag vs cable schedule BEFORE pulling

top_rework_causes_electrical.cause: Conduit routed to as-designed location but equipment orientation differs
top_rework_causes_electrical.prevention: Field-verify equipment/JB location BEFORE running conduit
Calibrated torque wrench, post torque tables at work area, verify lug matches conductor AND stud
Run calculation BEFORE pulling, account for cable OD not just gauge
Tension monitoring, intermediate pull points, never use backhoe to pull cable

top_rework_causes_electrical.cause: Wrong area classification — non-rated equipment in classified area
top_rework_causes_electrical.prevention: Mark classifications on site, carry area classification drawing, ASK if in doubt
Treat grounding as a system. Hammer-test Cadwelds (good = rings, bad = dull thud)
Stamp superseded drawings IMMEDIATELY. Use controlled document system.

top_rework_causes_electrical.cause: Failed megger after install — jacket damaged during pulling
top_rework_causes_electrical.prevention: Protect at penetrations with bushings, megger before AND after pulling

top_rework_causes_electrical.cause: Fire seals not installed or wrong type
top_rework_causes_electrical.prevention: Install as you go, use only specific listed firestop system

top_rework_causes_instrumentation.prevention: Verify nameplate against data sheet AND loop diagram before calibrating

top_rework_causes_instrumentation.cause: Tubing connected to wrong process tap
top_rework_causes_instrumentation.prevention: Verify tag on primary element, trace P&ID, confirm hookup drawing
PMI testing on SS if any doubt, segregate materials in warehouse

top_rework_causes_instrumentation.cause: Tube fitting leak at process connection
top_rework_causes_instrumentation.prevention: Train on proper Swagelok makeup, use gap gauges, 1-1/4 turn past finger tight
Standardize terminal markings, loop check every circuit end-to-end
Verify flow arrow on body matches P&ID flow direction
Liquid slopes toward transmitter, gas slopes toward process
Follow valve manufacturer air consumption data
Loop check verifies ENTIRE path: field device to DCS screen
REMOVE or ISOLATE all instruments before hydro/flush — on pre-test checklist

new_journeyman_wisdom: Your license says qualified. The field will teach you how much you still need to learn. Stay humble.
Learn to read P&IDs fluently — understand the process, not just your trade.
Always carry current drawings, a notebook, and a calibrated multimeter.
Never energize anything without second person verification. No exceptions.
When something doesn't match the drawing, STOP and ask. Don't 'make it work.'
Document everything. If it isn't written down, it didn't happen. Photos are your friend.
Know material codes. Wrong material in sour service can kill someone.
Treat every wire as energized until YOU verify it's dead with a known-good meter.
Relationships matter more than being right. The pipefitter foreman you work with today may give you access tomorrow.
Asking for help is not weakness. Installing something wrong because you were afraid to ask IS weakness.

foreman_tips.detail: Walk area, verify materials/drawings/permits/access. Planning = 7+ productive hours. Winging it = 4.
Hardest work first half when crew is fresh. Save cleanup/staging for end of day.
Skilled worker standing around = YOUR planning failure. Fix it and learn.
Pair best journeyman with greenest apprentice. Builds your bench.
If you can't measure it, you can't improve it or defend it when questioned.
Most interference issues resolved with 5-minute conversation if you have the relationship.
Designate one person as material coordinator. Journeymen should be installing, not walking to tool crib.

foreman_tips.tip: Safety is not separate from production
foreman_tips.detail: Shortcuts saving 10 minutes can cost 10 weeks if someone gets hurt.

toolbox_talk_format.frequency: Daily at shift start. Additional if scope/conditions change.
must_include: Specific hazards in today's work area

quality_walkdown_warning_signs: Cable tray covers missing — cables unprotected from sparks/debris
Junction boxes left open — moisture and pest ingress
Conduit ends not capped — debris damages cable during pull
Tubing without continuous support — sags and fails at vibration points
Instruments still in shipping plastic weeks later — not inspected or calibrated
Lack of cable/tube labeling — massive commissioning problem
Grounding not continuous — afterthought that creates safety issues
Outdoor enclosure seals not installed — water damage #1 cause of equipment failure
